text: Here, I am surrounded by the quiet hum of a modern space, the soft embrace of beanbags and the hush of muted colors. A lone figure is absorbed in their screen; there is a sense of contemplation and disconnection at once. The space seems designed for reflection or collaboration, yet right now it feels nearly empty, almost lonely. I am curious about the intentions behind such a setting—does its emptiness amplify my own thoughts, or does it simply echo a collective solitude?
